content.text:
Respond to the question:
Please share a choice you made in The Game of Life, and what factors you considered at that time.
回答問題：
請分享一次你在生命之旅遊戲裡做出的選擇，以及你當時考量了哪些因素。
content.assessment_standard:
10	A choice I made in the game was deciding whether to go to college or start working immediately. I considered factors like the immediate need for money versus the potential for a higher salary later. I chose college because I valued long-term stability and wanted access to better career paths, even if it meant taking on initial debt.|I had to choose whether to buy a very expensive, luxurious house or a modest one. I considered my current salary and the risk of drawing a bad event card later. I opted for the modest house to ensure I had enough cash for emergencies, prioritizing financial security over showing off.|I faced a choice of whether to change my career mid-game. I considered how much money my current job made versus the cost of going back to school. I decided to change because the new career offered higher long-term rewards. I weighed the short-term setback against the ultimate goal of winning.
9	A choice I made was deciding whether to go to college or start working immediately. I considered factors like immediate need for money versus potential for a higher salary later. I chose college because I valued long-term stability and better career paths, even with initial debt.|I had to choose whether to buy an expensive house or a modest one. I considered my current salary and the risk of drawing a bad event card. I opted for the modest house to ensure I had enough cash for emergencies, prioritizing financial security.|I faced a choice of whether to change my career mid-game. I considered my current job income versus the cost of going back to school. I decided to change because the new career offered higher long-term rewards, weighing short-term setback against winning.
8	A choice I make is deciding to go to college or start working immediate. I consider immediate need for money versus potential for higher salary. I choose college because I value long-term stability and better career paths, even with debt.|I have to choose buy expensive house or modest one. I consider current salary and risk of bad event card. I opt for modest house to ensure cash for emergencies, prioritize financial security.|I face choice to change career mid-game. I consider current job income versus cost go back school. I decide change because new career offer higher long-term rewards, weigh short-term setback against winning.
7	A choice is decide go college or work immediate. Consider need money versus potential high salary. Choose college because value long-term stability better career, even debt.|Choose buy expensive house or modest. Consider salary and risk bad event card. Opt modest house ensure cash for emergency, prioritize financial secure.|Face choice change career mid-game. Consider job income versus cost go school. Decide change new career offer high long-term reward, weigh short-term setback win.
6	A choice decide go college or work. Consider need money versus high salary. Choose college value long-term stability better career debt.|Choose buy expensive house modest. Consider salary risk bad event card. Opt modest house ensure cash emergency prioritize financial secure.|Face choice change career mid-game. Consider job income versus cost go school. Decide change new career high long-term reward weigh short-term setback.
5	Choice decide go college work. Consider money high salary. Choose college long-term stability career.|Choose buy expensive house modest. Consider salary risk bad event. Modest house cash emergency secure.|Choice change career mid-game. Consider income cost school. Change new career high reward setback.
4	Choice college work. Consider money salary. College stability.|Choose house modest. Salary risk. Cash emergency.|Change career. Income cost. High reward.
3	Choice college work. Money.|Choose house modest. Salary.|Change career. Income.
2	College work.|House modest.|Change career.
1	(No response / Silence)

content.text:
Respond to the question:
Please describe a recent situation where you had to prioritize your values (e.g., time, money, relationships, academics). How did you finally decide?
回答問題：
請描述最近一次你需要做「價值排序」的情況(例如：時間、金錢、人際、學業)，你最後是如何決定的？
content.assessment_standard:
10	Recently, I was invited to a weekend trip with friends, but I also had a major final exam on Monday. I had to prioritize between relationships and academics. I finally decided to skip the trip and study. I communicated my decision honestly to my friends, realizing that while friendships are crucial, securing my academic future was my top priority at that moment.|I had to choose between taking a higher-paying part-time job that required late nights or a lower-paying one with flexible hours. I prioritized my health and time over money. I chose the flexible job because getting enough sleep and having time for my hobbies is essential for my mental well-being.|When deciding how to spend my limited allowance, I had to choose between buying a new video game or saving up for a special gift for my mom's birthday. I prioritized my family relationship over my personal entertainment. I chose to save the money, knowing that making my mom happy would bring me more lasting joy.
9	Recently, I was invited to a trip with friends, but had a major final exam. I had to prioritize between relationships and academics. I decided to skip the trip and study. I communicated my decision to my friends, realizing securing my academic future was my top priority.|I had to choose between a higher-paying job requiring late nights or a lower-paying one with flexible hours. I prioritized health and time over money. I chose the flexible job because getting sleep and having time for hobbies is essential for my well-being.|When deciding how to spend allowance, I had to choose buying a video game or saving for a gift for my mom's birthday. I prioritized family relationship over personal entertainment. I chose to save the money, knowing making my mom happy brings lasting joy.
8	Recently, I am invited to trip with friends, but have major final exam. I have to prioritize relationships and academics. I decide skip trip and study. I communicate decision to friends, realize secure academic future is top priority.|I have to choose higher-paying job require late night or lower-paying with flexible hour. I prioritize health and time over money. I choose flexible job because get sleep and have time for hobbies is essential for well-being.|When decide spend allowance, I choose buy video game or save for gift for mom birthday. I prioritize family relationship over personal entertainment. I choose save money, know make mom happy bring lasting joy.
7	Recently invited trip friends, but have final exam. Prioritize relationship and academic. Decide skip trip study. Communicate decision friends, secure academic future top priority.|Choose higher-paying job late night or lower-paying flexible hour. Prioritize health time over money. Choose flexible job get sleep have time hobbies essential well-being.|Decide spend allowance, choose buy video game or save gift mom birthday. Prioritize family relationship over personal entertainment. Choose save money, make mom happy bring lasting joy.
6	Invited trip friends have final exam. Prioritize relationship academic. Decide skip trip study. Communicate decision friends secure academic future priority.|Choose high-paying job late night low-paying flexible hour. Prioritize health time money. Choose flexible job sleep time hobbies essential well-being.|Decide spend allowance buy video game save gift mom birthday. Prioritize family relationship personal entertainment. Choose save money make mom happy lasting joy.
5	Invited trip friends final exam. Prioritize relationship academic. Skip trip study. Academic future priority.|Choose high-paying late night flexible hour. Prioritize health time money. Flexible job sleep hobbies well-being.|Spend allowance video game gift mom. Prioritize family personal entertainment. Save money mom happy joy.
4	Trip friends final exam. Prioritize relationship academic. Skip trip study.|High-paying late flexible. Prioritize health time. Flexible job sleep.|Allowance video game gift mom. Prioritize family personal. Save money mom.
3	Trip final exam. Prioritize academic.|Job flexible. Prioritize health.|Allowance gift. Prioritize family.
2	Trip exam.|Job flexible.|Allowance gift.
1	(No response / Silence)

content.text:
Respond to the question:
When making decisions, which factor are you most likely to ignore? (e.g., long-term consequences, risks, impact on others) Why?
回答問題：
在做決定時，你覺得自己最容易忽略哪一項？(例如：長期後果、風險、他人影響) 為什麼？
content.assessment_standard:
10	When making decisions, I feel I most easily ignore long-term consequences. I am often tempted by immediate gratification, like playing games instead of studying. This short-sightedness can create bigger problems in the future. I need to train myself to pause and actively visualize where my current choices will lead me in a year's time.|I tend to ignore the impact my decisions have on other people. I get very focused on what I want or need in the moment, forgetting that my actions might inconvenience or hurt my friends or family. Developing more empathy and considering others' feelings before acting is an area I urgently need to work on.|Risk is the factor I most often overlook. I tend to be overly optimistic and assume everything will go perfectly according to plan. This leads me to make impulsive choices without a backup plan. I need to start asking myself 'what is the worst that could happen?' to make more balanced decisions.
9	When making decisions, I feel I most easily ignore long-term consequences. I am tempted by immediate gratification, like playing games instead of studying. This short-sightedness creates bigger problems in the future. I need to train myself to actively visualize where choices lead me.|I tend to ignore the impact my decisions have on other people. I get focused on what I want in the moment, forgetting actions might inconvenience friends or family. Developing empathy and considering others' feelings is an area I need to work on.|Risk is the factor I most often overlook. I am overly optimistic and assume everything will go perfectly. This leads to impulsive choices without a backup plan. I need to start asking myself 'what is the worst that could happen?' to make balanced decisions.
8	When make decisions, I feel I easily ignore long-term consequences. I am tempt by immediate gratification, like play games instead of study. This short-sightedness create bigger problems in future. I need train myself to visualize where choices lead me.|I tend to ignore impact my decisions have on other people. I get focus on what I want in moment, forgetting actions might inconvenience friends or family. Develop empathy and consider others feelings is area I need work on.|Risk is factor I most often overlook. I am overly optimistic and assume everything will go perfect. This lead to impulsive choices without backup plan. I need start asking 'what is worst happen?' to make balance decision.
7	When make decision, easily ignore long-term consequence. Tempt by immediate gratification, play game not study. Short-sightedness create big problem future. Need train visualize choice lead me.|Ignore impact decision have on other people. Focus what I want moment, forget action inconvenience friend family. Develop empathy consider other feeling area need work.|Risk is factor overlook. Overly optimistic assume everything go perfect. Lead impulsive choice without backup plan. Need ask 'what worst happen' make balance decision.
6	Make decision easily ignore long-term consequence. Tempt immediate gratification play game not study. Short-sightedness big problem future. Train visualize choice lead.|Ignore impact decision other people. Focus what want moment forget action inconvenience friend family. Develop empathy consider other feeling work.|Risk factor overlook. Overly optimistic assume everything perfect. Lead impulsive choice without backup plan. Ask worst happen balance decision.
5	Decision ignore long-term consequence. Tempt immediate gratification play game. Short-sightedness problem future. Train visualize choice.|Ignore impact decision other people. Focus want moment forget inconvenience friend. Develop empathy consider feeling.|Risk factor overlook. Overly optimistic assume perfect. Impulsive choice without backup. Ask worst happen.
4	Ignore long-term consequence. Immediate gratification game. Problem future. Visualize choice.|Ignore impact other people. Focus want moment. Empathy consider feeling.|Risk overlook. Optimistic perfect. Impulsive backup. Ask worst.
3	Ignore long-term consequence. Immediate gratification.|Ignore impact other. Empathy.|Risk overlook. Optimistic.
2	Long-term consequence.|Impact other.|Risk overlook.
1	(No response / Silence)

content.text:
Respond to the question:
Did the outcome of your life choices in the game differ from your initial expectations? How did you deal with it?
回答問題：
你在生命之旅遊戲中抉擇的結果與當初的期待是否有落差？你是如何處理的？
content.assessment_standard:
10	In the game, I chose a high-paying career expecting to win easily, but I kept drawing cards that made me pay huge taxes and bills. The outcome was vastly different from my expectation of being rich. I handled the disappointment by accepting that luck plays a huge role, both in the game and in real life, and focused on making the best of my remaining resources.|I expected that skipping college would give me a great head start in wealth. However, as the game progressed, players with degrees quickly surpassed my income. I was surprised and a bit frustrated. I dealt with it by changing my strategy mid-game, focusing on buying properties rather than relying solely on my salary.|I anticipated that buying a big house would secure my victory, but it ended up draining all my cash, leaving me vulnerable to unexpected expenses. The result was a stressful struggle. I managed my surprise by laughing at my own poor financial planning and using it as a humorous lesson in resource management.
9	In the game, I chose a high-paying career expecting to win easily, but I kept drawing cards to pay huge taxes. The outcome was vastly different from my expectation of being rich. I handled the disappointment by accepting that luck plays a huge role and focused on making the best of my resources.|I expected that skipping college would give me a head start in wealth. However, players with degrees quickly surpassed my income. I was surprised and frustrated. I dealt with it by changing my strategy mid-game, focusing on buying properties rather than relying on my salary.|I anticipated that buying a big house would secure my victory, but it drained all my cash, leaving me vulnerable to expenses. The result was a stressful struggle. I managed my surprise by laughing at my poor financial planning and using it as a lesson in resource management.
8	In the game, I choose high-paying career expecting to win easy, but I keep draw cards to pay huge taxes. The outcome is vastly different from expectation of being rich. I handle disappointment by accept that luck play huge role and focus on make best of resources.|I expect skip college give me head start in wealth. However, players with degrees quickly surpass my income. I am surprise and frustrate. I deal with it by change strategy mid-game, focus on buy properties rather than rely on salary.|I anticipate buy big house secure victory, but it drain all my cash, leave me vulnerable to expenses. Result is stressful struggle. I manage surprise by laugh at poor financial plan and use it as lesson in resource management.
7	In game, I choose high-pay career expect win easy, but I draw card pay huge tax. Outcome different from expect rich. I handle disappoint accept luck play huge role focus make best resource.|Expect skip college give head start wealth. However player with degree surpass income. I surprise frustrate. Deal with it change strategy mid-game focus buy property not rely salary.|Anticipate buy big house secure victory, but drain all cash leave vulnerable expense. Result stressful struggle. Manage surprise laugh poor financial plan use lesson resource manage.
6	Choose high-pay career expect win easy draw card pay huge tax. Outcome different expect rich. Handle disappoint accept luck play huge role focus best resource.|Expect skip college head start wealth. Player with degree surpass income. Surprise frustrate. Deal change strategy mid-game focus buy property not rely salary.|Anticipate buy big house secure victory drain all cash leave vulnerable expense. Result stressful struggle. Manage surprise laugh poor financial plan lesson resource manage.
5	Choose high-pay career expect win draw card pay tax. Outcome different expect rich. Disappoint accept luck focus best resource.|Expect skip college head start wealth. Degree surpass income. Surprise frustrate. Change strategy mid-game buy property.|Anticipate big house secure victory drain cash vulnerable expense. Stressful struggle. Laugh poor financial plan lesson manage.
4	Choose high-pay career expect win. Outcome different. Disappoint accept luck.|Expect skip college wealth. Degree surpass income. Change strategy buy property.|Anticipate big house victory drain cash. Stressful struggle. Laugh financial plan.
3	Choose career expect win. Outcome different.|Skip college wealth. Surpass income.|Big house victory. Drain cash.
2	Expect win. Different.|College wealth. Income.|Big house. Cash.
1	(No response / Silence)

content.text:
Respond to the question:
If you could restart the game, what different career or life path would you choose to experience, and why?
回答問題：
如果你能重玩一次生命之旅遊戲，你會選擇體驗什麼不同的職業或生活路徑？為什麼？
content.assessment_standard:
10	If I could restart the game, I would choose the path of an artist or musician instead of a doctor. During my first playthrough, I focused strictly on making money, which was safe but a bit boring. I would choose a creative path to experience a more unpredictable and adventurous life, just to see how differently the game's events would affect me.|I would definitely choose to go to college first if I replayed. I tried the 'work immediately' route and struggled with a low salary the whole game. I want to experience the long-term benefits of investing in education early on. It would teach me how patience and initial sacrifice can lead to greater success later.|If I restarted, I would take more risks and choose action-packed life paths, like a police officer or athlete. I played too cautiously last time and avoided all the exciting but risky spaces. I want to experience the thrill of high stakes and see if bold choices can lead to a more spectacular victory.
9	If I could restart the game, I would choose the path of an artist or musician instead of a doctor. I focused strictly on making money, which was safe but boring. I would choose a creative path to experience an unpredictable and adventurous life, to see how events affect me.|I would definitely choose to go to college first if I replayed. I tried the 'work immediately' route and struggled with a low salary. I want to experience the long-term benefits of investing in education early on. It would teach me how initial sacrifice leads to greater success.|If I restarted, I would take more risks and choose action-packed life paths, like a police officer or athlete. I played too cautiously last time and avoided risky spaces. I want to experience the thrill of high stakes and see if bold choices lead to a spectacular victory.
8	If I could restart game, I would choose path of artist or musician instead of doctor. I focus strictly on making money, which is safe but boring. I choose creative path to experience unpredictable and adventurous life, to see how events affect me.|I would definitely choose go to college first if I replay. I try 'work immediate' route and struggle with low salary. I want to experience long-term benefits of invest in education early. It teach me how initial sacrifice lead to greater success.|If I restart, I would take more risks and choose action-packed life paths, like police officer or athlete. I play too cautious last time and avoid risky spaces. I want experience thrill of high stakes and see if bold choices lead to spectacular victory.
7	If restart game, I choose path artist or musician instead doctor. Focus make money safe but boring. Choose creative path experience unpredictable adventurous life see how event affect me.|Definitely choose go college first if replay. Try work immediate route struggle low salary. Want experience long-term benefit invest education early. Teach initial sacrifice lead greater success.|If restart, take more risk choose action-packed life path like police athlete. Play too cautious last time avoid risky space. Want experience thrill high stakes see bold choice lead spectacular victory.
6	Restart game choose path artist musician instead doctor. Focus make money safe boring. Choose creative path experience unpredictable adventurous life see event affect me.|Choose go college first replay. Try work immediate route struggle low salary. Want experience long-term benefit invest education early. Teach sacrifice lead greater success.|Restart take more risk choose action-packed life path police athlete. Play cautious last time avoid risky space. Want experience thrill high stakes bold choice lead spectacular victory.
5	Restart game choose artist musician doctor. Focus make money safe boring. Creative path unpredictable adventurous life.|Choose college first replay. Work immediate struggle low salary. Experience long-term benefit invest education. Sacrifice lead greater success.|Restart take risk choose action-packed police athlete. Play cautious avoid risky space. Experience thrill high stakes bold choice.
4	Restart choose artist musician. Make money safe boring. Creative unpredictable adventurous.|Choose college first. Work immediate struggle. Long-term benefit education.|Restart take risk action-packed. Play cautious avoid. Thrill high stakes.
